bullets.text: Lebanon reports 39 casualties from Israeli strikes, as exchanges of fire continue despite a ceasefire agreement reached last month.

Russian President Putin used his Victory Day speech to justify the ongoing 'special military operation' in Ukraine, while Iran accused the US of 'reckless military adventure' following recent attacks.
Four men from south Florida have been convicted in Miami federal court for their involvement in the 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moïse.
UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer faces a leadership challenge following a local election defeat, as a former minister announced intentions to contend for the top position.
A new analysis reveals that 14 homeless individuals die annually in Australian public parks or rural areas, prompting calls for increased investment in the upcoming budget.
The Coalition has criticized Chris Minns after tech giants snubbed an inquiry, with a court ruling potentially weakening parliament's power and making inquiries ineffective.
The US has imposed sanctions on Chinese companies, accusing them of providing satellite imagery to Iran that enabled strikes against American forces in the Middle East.
China's exports have surged by 14% ahead of an upcoming summit between President Trump and Xi Jinping, indicating that US tariffs have had minimal impact on China's manufacturing sector.

Health officials are downplaying the risk of a new pandemic from a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship near Tenerife, with the World Health Organization stating the incident is not likely to trigger a global health crisis.
